Good, .NET 10 SDK is available. Let me create a C# MCP server project. I'll use the official `ModelContextProtocol` NuGet package (the C# SDK for MCP). Let me set up the project structure.

The key components:
1. A console app with stdio transport
2. An MCP tool that uses HttpClient to call an external API
3. A prompt template for summarization
4. Logging redirected to stderr so it doesn't interfere with JSON-RPC on stdout

Let me create the project.

        <member name="M:System.IdHelpers.CreateMonotonicId(System.DateTimeOffset)">
            <summary>
            Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks
            and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
            </summary>
            <param name="timestamp">The timestamp to embed in the identifier.</param>
            <returns>A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.</returns>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values:
            - High 64 bits: <see cref="P:System.DateTimeOffset.Ticks"/> from the timestamp
            - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter
            </para>
            <para>
            The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically,
            which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike <c>Guid.CreateVersion7</c>,
            which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees
            strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>

            Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks
            and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
            </summary>
            <param name="timestamp">The timestamp to embed in the identifier.</param>
            <returns>A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.</returns>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values:
            - High 64 bits: <see cref="P:System.DateTimeOffset.Ticks"/> from the timestamp
            - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter
            </para>
            <para>
            The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically,
            which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike <c>Guid.CreateVersion7</c>,
            which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees
            strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.
            </para>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the API. Let me create the files:

1. `Program.cs` - Host setup with stdio transport, logging to stderr
2. `Tools/ApiTools.cs` - Tool class with HttpClient DI
3. `Prompts/SummarizationPrompts.cs` - Prompt template

Key points:
- Use `WithStdioServerTransport()` for stdio
- Use `[McpServerToolType]` and `[McpServerTool]` attributes
- Use `[McpServerPromptType]` and `[McpServerPrompt]` attributes


- I need to make sure logging goes to stderr so the JSON-RPC messages on stdout stay clean

+using System.ComponentModel;
+using ModelContextProtocol.Server;
+
+namespace McpServer.Prompts;
+
+[McpServerPromptType]
+public sealed class SummarizationPrompts
+{
+    [McpServerPrompt, Description("Generates a concise summary of the provided text.")]
+    public static IEnumerable<ChatMessage> Summarize(
+        [Description("The text content to summarize.")] string content,
+        [Description("Desired detail level: brief, standard, or detailed.")] string detailLevel = "standard")
+    {
+        yield return new ChatMessage(
+            ChatRole.User,
+            $$"""
+            Summarize the following text at a {{detailLevel}} level of detail.
+            Return only the summary with no preamble.
+
+            ---
+            {{content}}
+            ---
+            """);
+    }
+}
+
